title: Creamy Chicken Tagliatelle Recipe
summary: This creamy chicken tagliatelle recipe will hit all of the nostalgia feels. We’ve made ours slightly fancier by making your own pesto – because that will make this dish a million times more delicious. Explore the comforting richness of our chicken tagliatelle recipe, where tender chicken meets luscious, creamy pasta in a deliciously satisfying dish. This chicken tagliatelle combines the delicate texture of tagliatelle with a velvety sauce made from fresh cream, homemade pesto, and Parmesan cheese, creating a delightful meal perfect for any occasion. This recipe is a delicious twist on classic Italian pasta dishes. Whether you're preparing a cosy Sunday lunch or a special dinner, this creamy chicken pasta is sure to impress. With just a few simple ingredients and straightforward cooking techniques, you can create a restaurant-quality dish right at home. Ready to elevate your pasta game? Try this irresistible chicken tagliatelle and enjoy a taste of Italy in every bite.

method.description: Tip the pine nuts into a small frying pan set over a medium heat and toast, tossing really regularly until evenly golden brown and smelling amazing. Tip into a high-powered blender and leave to cool for 5 mins.

Peel the garlic clove and add to the blender along with three-quarters of the basil, then finely grate the Parmesan and add this along with the lemon zest and juice. Spoon in 3 tbsp olive oil, add a pinch of salt and pepper, then whizz until super smooth. Add a few tablespoons of cold water if necessary. Alternatively, whizz in a bowl with a stick blender. Set the pesto aside.

Cut the chicken into bite-sized pieces and season really well with salt and pepper. Cut the courgette in half lengthways on a clean chopping board, then slice into ½cm half-moons. Cut the cherry tomatoes in half.

Heat a splash of oil in a medium frying pan over a high heat. Add the chicken and courgette pieces, then fry for 5-6 mins until caramelised and the courgette is softening. Chuck in the halved tomatoes and fry for 1 min. Pour in the double cream, then spoon in three-quarters of the pesto. Simmer for 1 min, then season with cracked black pepper.
Meanwhile, cook the tagliatelle in a large pan of boiling salted water following pack instructions.
Use tongs to transfer the cooked pasta straight into the frying pan. Toss really well in the sauce, adding a little of the pasta cooking water to loosen.
Divide between bowls, then top with the remaining basil leaves, the remaining pesto and finally a grating of Parmesan.

chefs.name: Adam Bush
chefs.summary: Adam is a chef, baker and food writer with a love of big, bold flavours. He trained in Michelin-starred kitchens, pubs and a bakery before turning his hand to recipe writing.
